FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Regional consolidation heating up amid strong earnings backdrop, while Fed rate-hike odds climb and CFPB leadership transition unfolds.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian acquiring TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks Hawaii's biggest bank returning to mainland U.S. after BNP Paribas exit.
Q2 bank earnings poised to be strong on SpaceX IPO advisory fees, geopolitical volatility trading, and commercial lending pickup—directly offsetting NIM pressure if Fed hikes in July.
Fed rate-hike odds for July rising on Strait of Hormuz oil spike; signals potential deposit-pricing and NIM compression headwinds ahead.
Trump CFPB nominee Brian Johnson wins 60-day post-confirmation review on staff-cut plan—gives him discretion over bureau workforce reduction.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Russell Investments joins CalPERS in major nonprofit pivot; RIA consolidation accelerates across platforms; Betterment faces cash sweep litigation.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquires Russell Investments for $580B in assets under management, converting to nonprofit structure and eliminating debt—a Vanguard-style repositioning play.
RIA consolidation hits overdrive: Hightower posts record organic growth, Choreo closes fourth acquisition post-SpaceX, and ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform.
Summitry ($4B RIA, Aspen-backed) elevates two co-CEOs as founder steps to executive chairman after 22 years—typical PE playbook succession.
Betterment hit with class action over cash sweep tactics—clients claim firm quietly moved cash into non-interest-bearing accounts while capturing spreads.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: U.S. P&C industry posts decade-best underwriting results, but geopolitical risk in Hormuz Strait threatens margin gains; meanwhile, Saudi Re expands Lloyd's reach and new entrants target California's homeowners void.

bullets.text: $84B in underwriting gains over two years with lowest combined ratio in a decade—pricing discipline paying off across the U.S. P&C market, though performance varies sharply by line of business.
Supertanker attack in Hormuz Strait (handling 21% of global crude) will spike war-risk insurance premiums and create P&L volatility for marine underwriters in a critical corridor.
Saudi Re deepens Lloyd's presence with 22.5% stake in AdA Risk (£8.95M), signaling appetite for London market expansion with regulatory green light already in hand.
RockRose Risk launches AI-driven homeowners product in California tied to wildfire mitigation—targeting the market gap left by traditional carriers retreating from the state.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Mastercard explores Vocalink exit while CMBS distress signals rise; fintech faces headwinds in Europe as Nubank expands in Mexico.

bullets.text: Mastercard weighing Vocalink sale—UK payments platform acquired from British banks in 2016 now potentially returning to original owners, signaling strategic portfolio realignment.
CMBS special servicing rates climbed in June 2026 on distressed large retail, office, and lodging loans—credit deterioration likely to drive servicer workouts across property-backed securitizations.
Klarna sued in Dutch class action while Nubank secures Mexican banking license—mixed fintech fortunes with regulatory risk in Europe offsetting geographic expansion wins.
North America remains LP-favored for private debt despite market noise; AlbaCore data shows widening divergence between performing and distressed credit performance.

PE & Deals.lede: Arctos pioneers a novel GP financing structure while secondaries market heats up with LP-led deals and new advisor muscle.

bullets.text: Arctos closes $6.2B GP financing using 'self-liquidating' Keystone strategy—a first for this structure in GP-financing, allowing capital returns while maintaining portfolio exposure.
Moelis poaches four-person Evercore secondaries team as MD to build LP-led practice, betting on this segment as sellers capitalize on favorable pricing and liquidity.
Korean pension KBIZ allocating ~$230M to domestic blind-pool PE/VC funds in 2026, reflecting institutional capital shift toward Korea-focused managers.

Markets & Macro.lede: Bank earnings season opens amid QT structural risks, while small caps rally and central banks test new frontiers in digital currency and fiscal headwinds.

bullets.text: Bank earnings kick off Tuesday with high expectations; market braced for disappointment as major lenders report quarterly results.
QE structurally rewired bank balance sheets and funding models, making QT riskier than policymakers assume due to continued central bank liquidity dependence.
Russell 2000 on track for best year since 2003 as mega-cap repricing drives rotation into small-cap AI capex and tax-policy beneficiaries.
UK 10-year gilt yields exceed 5% on oil surge and fiscal concerns just as new PM takes office; ECB names 36 banks—including skeptics—to pilot digital euro in 2025.

Newsletters.lede: Big bank earnings tomorrow set tone for credit quality and consumer health; meanwhile, ETF redemptions signal market caution and advisory workflow gets a tax efficiency upgrade.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—key window into deposit dynamics, credit quality, and consumer health across lending, trading, and wealth management.
Rare week of negative ETF flows signals investor pullback; redemptions hit equity, bond, and alternative products as market caution spreads.
Vanguard and Envestnet partner on tax-friction tools for model portfolio conversions—addresses key RIA workflow pain point in advisor ecosystem.
Student loan policy shifts are forcing advisors to rebuild college planning strategies, reshaping demand for education financing and advisory services.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Four takes on incentive alignment and competitive advantage: NBA players gaming metrics, open-source AI disrupting moats, computational complexity preserving competition, and a Groq founder's $20B NVIDIA pivot.

bullets.text: Open-source AI models now match frontier capabilities at 1/10th the cost, forcing founders to compete on moats beyond model performance—the raw compute advantage game is over.
Groq's Jonathan Ross secured a $20B NVIDIA partnership by navigating near-bankruptcy with creative financing (Grok Bonds)—a case study in startup resilience when conventional capital dries up.
Tyler Cowen's insight: competitive markets exist if and only if P ≠ NP; if collusion detection becomes computationally intractable, cartels collapse and competition wins as equilibrium.

all.lede: Regional bank M&A accelerates with First Hawaiian-TriCo deal, while major bank earnings arrive tomorrow amid QT concerns.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian Bank acquiring TriCo Bancshares for $2B—marks Hawaiian bank's return to mainland after BNP Paribas exit, closing by year-end.
Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow; results will signal credit quality, deposit trends, and consumer health across the economy.
Mastercard exploring Vocalink sale—potentially returning UK payments platform to original British bank owners a decade after acquisition.
QT poses hidden risks: QE structurally reshaped bank balance sheets and funding models, making quantitative tightening more dangerous than policymakers realize.
